name: Red Lentil Dal
tagline: Silky red lentil dal with ginger, turmeric, and ghee tadka
highlight: Pantry dinner — lentils, water, spices, ghee, done

hero.alt: Red lentil dal with ghee tadka and cilantro
hero.caption: red lentil dal — golden, simple

healthFacts: Red lentils need no soaking and cook in 15 min — fastest legume around.
Tadka (ghee infused with spices) carries fat-soluble curcumin into the body.

steps.title: Boil lentils with turmeric
steps.detail: Rinse lentils. Combine with 4 cups water, turmeric, salt. Bring to a boil and simmer 15 min, skimming foam, until lentils break down.
steps.tip: Foam is starch — skim or stir back in. Either is fine.

steps.title: Whisk to silk
steps.detail: Whisk dal vigorously off heat — should look like loose mashed potato. Thin with hot water if needed.
steps.tip: An immersion blender 2-second pulse makes it restaurant-smooth.

steps.title: Build the tadka
steps.detail: Heat ghee in a small pan. Add cumin and mustard seeds, sizzle 20 sec. Add onion, cook 4 min until edges brown. Stir in garlic, ginger, tomato; cook 2 min.
steps.tip: Tadka should sound like rain when it hits the dal — that's the flavor explosion.

steps.title: Pour and rest
steps.detail: Pour tadka over the dal — listen for the sizzle. Stir most of it in, leave some on top for the visual. Cilantro and lime to finish.
steps.tip: Eat with rice or roti. Improves overnight.
